patch.value: Scale benchmark harness: synthetic 10k/100k/1M-item workspace generator + latency/memory/token baseline for the read and claim hot paths

Do-first measurement child of pm-9rxu (mirrors the pm-a22j token-baseline pattern from pm-n7rr): no optimization lands without before/after numbers from this harness. Duplicate check: no existing benchmark/perf-harness item covers workspace-size scaling (pm-4oww measured extension latency only).

Repeatable script/harness generates synthetic workspaces at 10k/100k/1M items (realistic type/status/tag/parent distribution, history streams included); measures p50/p95 wall time, peak RSS, and output token count for context/next/list/search/get/create/claim; baseline numbers recorded on the epic (pm notes) and in a committed report; harness runs from a clean checkout without touching the real workspace

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Slicing (S1-S4), performance-program entry point per pm-9rxu delivery order step 1.

S1 generator (~150m): deterministic seeded synthetic-workspace generator at 10k/100k/1M items. Shape distributions derived from THIS workspace (~1.7k items) scaled up: type mix, status partition (~85% closed at maturity), dep-graph fan-in/out, tag cardinality, history-stream lengths, body sizes. Two write modes: SDK-path (exercises real mutation code, honest but slow - fine to 10k) and direct-layout fast mode for 100k/1M (must produce byte-identical layout; verify with pm validate). Lives in scripts/bench/, runs from clean checkout, never touches the real workspace (withTempPmPath pattern - realpath the root, see pm-dprb).

S2 measurement runner (~120m): per-tier runs of the hot paths (context/next/list/search/get/create/claim) capturing p50/p95 wall time, peak RSS, and output token count (reuse the token-counting approach from the closed pm-cu1i gate). Cold vs warm split matters: first-invocation cost vs memoized reads (~2x warm-read effect, PR#421). Emit one JSON report artifact per tier.

S3 published baseline (~60m): commit the report under docs/ + record headline numbers as a note on pm-9rxu; declare per-tier budgets (e.g. context p95 and token ceiling at 1M items) - budgets are the epic's success metric, numbers before optimization.

S4 regression gate (~120m): 10k tier in PR CI (seconds), 100k/1M tiers nightly; fail on budget regression beyond tolerance band. Nightly is the sole oracle for the big tiers (same pattern as the macOS matrix gap, GH-510).

Sequencing: S1+S2 are spike-able now with zero deps; S3/S4 gate everything downstream (pm-801d index, pm-dfg0 pagination, pm-0qg5 fan-out, pm-lvyd claim throughput all need before/after numbers). Risk: synthetic-shape realism - mitigated by deriving distributions from the real workspace rather than inventing them.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
S2 measurement-target extension (2026-07-11 perf brainstorm; ledger note on pm-9rxu). Beyond the hot-path list (context/next/list/search/get/create/claim), the S2 runner should also capture, per tier:
- Governance reads: validate, health, deps --summary, aggregate, stats wall time — agent-facing too, and health has silently 2x'd before (pm-c90s).
- Per-phase attribution via the EXISTING OTEL span surface: startup (compile-cache cold vs warm), settings read, workspace scan/parse, serialize, extension activation. Watch pm-25se (exporter can hold the process open).
- Git subprocess count + cumulative subprocess wall time per command (named in the program map).
- Semantic/BM25 index refresh throughput: time-to-fresh after one mutation and after bulk generation — background-refresh queue behavior at 100k+ items is unknown.
- Extension residual: identical command bare vs with the 11 bundled packages (shipped pm-4oww fix left ~150ms residual).
- MCP warm-process vs CLI cold-start delta: same operation via MCP tool call vs subprocess — quantifies the sanctioned warm path (ADR pm-i7i4 forbids a daemon).
- Output token counts are a first-class perf metric for the agent path (tokens = latency), not just the pm-cu1i gate reused.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Session start 2026-07-12 (maintainer directive: drastically improve CLI+SDK performance, zero feature loss — EXECUTION session, follows the 2026-07-11 brainstorm ledger on pm-9rxu). Claimed by maintainer-agent. Plan: S1 generator + S2 runner built this session (pi sub-agent lanes), S3 baseline published, then measured quick-win levers (git-subprocess diet / bundle minify / extension residual) strictly with harness before/after. Session-anchored timings at 1.7k items, warm cache: list 1.4s, get 1.2s, next 1.6s, context 1.8s, search 2.0s, validate 10.4s; startup floor is ~90ms (node dist/cli.js --version == bare node) so the entire budget is per-command read-path work, confirming the ledger's "startup is near-floor" finding.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Phase attribution of the read path (2026-07-12 session, real workspace ~1.7k items, warm fs cache, node 26.4.0, production install = dist/cli.js -> cli-bundle). Method: wall-clock timing + node --cpu-prof + experimental esbuild variants in scratchpad (never committed).

BASELINE (production, pm <cmd> --json): list 1392ms / get 1230ms / next 1748ms / context 1822ms / search 2027ms / validate 10444ms. Second (warm) runs are NOT faster (list 2033ms) — the per-process cost repeats every invocation; PR#421 memoization is in-process only.

DECOMPOSITION of pm list ~1400ms:
1. Startup floor ~90ms: node dist/cli.js --version == bare node -e '' (94ms). Fast-version path + V8 compile cache working as designed. Confirms ledger: startup shaving is done.
2. ESM module loading ~850-950ms (60-65%): cpu-prof shows 1251ms of profile self-time in node-internal (esm/utils compile 187ms, esm/loader 155ms, package_json_reader 120ms, esm/resolve 88ms, url 75ms) vs only ~200ms in app chunks. Root cause: scripts/bundle-cli.mjs line 133 `packages: "external"` (per ADR pm-irp1) + src/core/sentry/instrument.ts does STATIC `import * as SentryModule from "@sentry/node"` -> the whole @sentry/node -> OTel -> undici forest is ESM-resolved+compiled from node_modules/.pnpm on EVERY command, even when PM_SENTRY_DISABLED=1 (the disable check runs after the import already happened). Isolated measurement: `await import("@sentry/node")` alone = 847ms; `commander` = 10ms.
3. Command work (scan 1.7k items + filter + serialize + extension activation) ~450-650ms. --no-extensions saves only ~100ms. Zero git subprocesses spawned during pm list (PATH-shim count = 0) — the git-diet lever is a NO-OP for pure reads at current N; renderers never call git on this path.

EXPERIMENTAL VARIANTS (same source, esbuild variants in scratchpad, output verified byte-identical to production except the `now` timestamp):
A. deps-bundled-except-sentry (external @sentry/node): 1382-1603ms — NO WIN. Bundling everything else buys nothing; sentry IS the module-load cost.
B. all-deps-bundled incl sentry (needs createRequire banner — @opentelemetry/instrumentation has a CJS dynamic require that crashes the naive bundle): 890-1005ms. ~450ms win but contradicts ADR pm-irp1 external-deps decision; not pursued this session.
C. production layout + @sentry/node aliased to empty stub (= perfect lazy-import ceiling for telemetry-disabled runs): 562-801ms. THE lever: ~600-800ms (~55%) off every command, zero output change.

CONCLUSION: the dominant CLI wall-time lever at small N is lazy-loading @sentry/node (dynamic import inside ensureSentryInit, after the isSentryDisabled gate). instrument.ts is the only importer and already funnels through async ensureSentryInit()/getSentry(), so the refactor is contained. Remaining ~500-650ms is genuine read-path work = exactly what the S1/S2 harness + pm-801d index own.

author: maintainer-agent
message: Begin implementation of the scale generator, benchmark runner, published baseline, and regression budgets

value.note: local benchmark operation and regression policy

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Completion evidence: deterministic direct-layout and SDK workspace generation, CLI+SDK runner, p50/p95/RSS/token reporting, 10k local regression budget, and committed 10k/100k reports are implemented. PM-linked tests passed: 13 harness tests plus benchmark:scale:check at 10k. Manual 100k run completed in an isolated temp workspace; CLI list/get/next/context/search/create/claim measured 1855/1325/3470/3116/2941/723/692ms, SDK 923/440/2563/2587/2591/8/7ms, and every payload stayed below 5000 estimated tokens. Generator never targeted the real tracker.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Short regression samples must gate a best-observed floor plus a documented scheduler/filesystem margin; using three-sample p95 (the maximum) made the local gate host-load-sensitive. Product reports still retain p95, while 20+ samples gate p95 directly.

patch.value: Implemented and locally verified deterministic 10k/100k/1M-capable scale generation, CLI+SDK measurement, committed baselines, and the 10k regression gate; PM-linked tests and isolated 100k dogfood passed.
Delivered deterministic scale generation, local CLI/SDK benchmark reporting, committed 10k/100k evidence, and an honest local regression budget.
Repeatably generate isolated 10k/100k/1M-shaped trackers and measure latency, RSS, and token cost without touching the repository tracker.
The generator and runner cover both SDK and direct-layout modes; PM-linked 10k gates and isolated 100k dogfood passed with reports committed.

author: maintainer-agent

author: maintainer-agent
message: Address PR #525 benchmark correctness and 1M generation findings

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Review findings accepted for implementation: child-spawn failure must terminate the RSS sampler; generator should avoid constructing the 1M corpus twice by creating type directories on demand. Duplicate check reused this canonical benchmark item.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: PR #525 review-fix evidence: RSS sampling is now try/finally-cleaned on real spawn ENOENT; the generator eliminates the O(N) document prepass by deriving its finite built-in type directories directly. Focused benchmark/generator tests passed, local 10k CLI+SDK budget passed, 88-command installed-package dogfood passed, and full coverage is 100/100/100/100 across 5588 tests.

patch.value: PR #525 benchmark review fixes implemented and locally verified: spawn cleanup, single-pass fixture generation, scale budget, package dogfood, and 100% coverage passed.
